name: toggle
title: Toggle
description: A two-state button that stays on while pressed, built on Base UI's Toggle. Renders a native button exposing aria-pressed, toggles with Enter/Space, and signals the pressed state with a filled background PLUS a pressed-in position/border cue so it is never color-only and survives forced-colors mode. cva size and variant tokens shared with Button, 44px targets on both axes, and a dev guard that requires an accessible name on icon-only toggles.
useWhen: An in-context two-state press whose state stays visible (Bold/Italic, Show map, Grid vs list view)
A single formatting or filter control that turns on and off in place
Toolbar buttons that reflect a persistent pressed state
avoidWhen: A selection or consent submitted with a form (use a Checkbox)
A setting that takes effect immediately as an on/off row with a label (use a Switch)
Choosing exactly one option from a set (use a radio-group or a single-selection Toggle group)
Triggering a one-off action with no persistent state (use a Button)

keyboard: Tab moves focus to the toggle; Shift+Tab moves away
Enter or Space toggles between pressed and not pressed
A disabled toggle is removed from the tab order and cannot toggle
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default on both axes (min-h-11 and min-w-11 keep an icon-only toggle at least 44x44px)
notes: The control renders a native button exposing aria-pressed; the accessible name comes from its text content or, for icon-only toggles, a required aria-label plus a matching tooltip. A dev-only guard warns when no accessible name is present.

Non-color redundancy: the pressed state nudges the content in (translate-y position cue) and switches the border to the primary token, so on/off stays distinguishable in Windows High Contrast / forced-colors mode where the pressed fill color is overridden.
Every variant keeps a visible border so forced-colors mode paints a boundary; the content nudge is gated behind motion-safe.
translate-y is a block-axis transform, so it needs no RTL mirror; all spacing uses logical properties.
